operational_definition: Systemic therapy administered during the RT course or on an RT treatment day
required_user_input: Last dose date; next dose date; schedule
default_app_interpretation: Highest likelihood of overlapping acute toxicity or radiosensitisation
escalation_logic: Apply agent-specific concurrent warning and combination rules

operational_definition: Systemic therapy stopped before RT but residual pharmacologic/biologic effect may remain
default_app_interpretation: Use agent-specific washout when available; otherwise classify by half-life/biologic persistence
escalation_logic: Escalate if within known hold interval, unresolved toxicity or long biologic effect

operational_definition: Systemic therapy planned soon after RT while acute tissue injury is evolving
default_app_interpretation: Assess wound healing, marrow recovery, mucosal recovery and radiation recall potential
escalation_logic: Escalate if restart precedes expected recovery or agent has recall/wound-healing concern

operational_definition: Systemic therapy and RT separated beyond immediate overlap interval
default_app_interpretation: Usually lower concern, but late recall and persistent organ toxicity remain possible
escalation_logic: Apply residual-risk warning where agent has long half-life or delayed toxicity

operational_definition: Systemic therapy not yet started but intended after RT
default_app_interpretation: Generate restart/start guidance and required monitoring
escalation_logic: Escalate for anti-VEGF, strong myelosuppression, immune toxicity or wound-healing concern

default_app_interpretation: Do not downgrade risk; show uncertainty warning
escalation_logic: Require clinician confirmation before final output
